title: B002: Implement Kubernetes-interacting packages
summary: All five issues from the greptile-apps[bot] external review are correctly addressed: (1) re-fetch after c.Update in EnsureDeployment, EnsureDatabase, ensureUser, ensureGrant, and EnsureCertificate to avoid evaluating stale pre-update status; (2) ObservedGeneration guard added to IsDeploymentReady; (3) NodePort preservation in EnsureService matching by Port+Protocol; (4) owner reference verification in CreateImmutableConfigMap on AlreadyExists path. Tests added for all new behaviors. All unit tests pass, go vet clean, golangci-lint clean, gosec clean.

tests_checklist.item: All existing unit tests pass (config, deployment packages)
Existing TestIsDeploymentReady_true updated with matching Generation/ObservedGeneration
Edge cases covered (mismatched owner UID, generation lag with full replicas)

code_quality_checklist.item: Re-fetch pattern applied consistently across all 5 Ensure* functions that evaluate readiness
Functions that return only error (EnsurePushSecret, EnsureCronJob, EnsureService) correctly omit re-fetch
ObservedGeneration guard scoped to Deployment (external CRDs may not expose it)

security_checklist.item: Owner reference check prevents acting on resources owned by another controller
No secrets exposed in error messages

architecture_checklist.item: Re-fetch pattern is the simplest correct fix for stale-status bug
NodePort preservation matches Port+Protocol (standard K8s matching semantics)
Owner check uses metav1.GetControllerOf consistent with SetControllerReference usage

dry_yagni_checklist.item: Changes are minimal and scoped to the reported issues

fail_fast_checklist.item: Re-fetch errors return immediately with wrapped error
Owner mismatch returns error immediately (not silently ignored)

defensive_checklist.item: controllerRef nil check before UID comparison in CreateImmutableConfigMap
ObservedGeneration guard prevents false-positive readiness during rollout

suggested_improvements: D2 (minor): In EnsureService NodePort preservation (deployment.go:79-88), if the caller omits Protocol (zero value "") but the existing port has Protocol "TCP" (set by API server), the Port+Protocol match will fail and the NodePort won't be preserved. Consider falling back to Port-only matching when Protocol is empty, or document that callers must always set Protocol.

detected_patterns.name: Re-fetch after update before readiness evaluation
detected_patterns.applies_when: Writing an Ensure* function that updates an existing Kubernetes resource and then evaluates its readiness status (returns (bool, error) where bool indicates readiness)
detected_patterns.description: After calling c.Update on an existing resource, the in-memory object retains the status from the initial c.Get (pre-update). If readiness is evaluated against this stale status, it can return false-positive ready=true when the controller has not yet processed the spec change. The fix is to c.Get the object again after c.Update, before calling the Is*Ready function. Functions that only return error (no readiness evaluation) do not need the re-fetch.
